author: jsign
content: The `NewPayloadRequest` is something defined in consensus-specs such that the hash_tree_root matches what the CL expects, not strictly EngineAPI definitions

author: jsign
content: This was a recent fix here https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2761

author: jsign
content: lmk if htis makes sense to you

author: 0xrubo
content: to me, it would make more sense to keep zkevm input aligned with the engine api ssz format, so we use the same code

author: 0xrubo
content: so far, this is the only one diverging

author: 0xrubo
content: if engine api and zkevm data structures are gonna diverge more, that is one thing. if not, better to keep aligned.

author: jsign
content: Yeah they not being exactly the same is some tension that if you want to simplify the EL, you will ask the CL to have the other view for public input verification (since symmetrically they should know hte extra engine api format). so i think there might not be a free lunch solution

author: jsign
content: cc <@402080271563751435> if he might have an opinion from the CL side reg specs... mostly reg `NewPayloadRequest` might survive only for hte context of proving, so maybe there is some angle of potential justification on which side could be more flexible. although all this is might be quite subjective

author: jsign
content: i agree that the only field that has this divergence is the current one, maybe we can leave things as they are since is not a big difference for ELs to handle. but still agree worth raising this point to be aware; and maybe at some fork boundary the diff might be bigger and we can reevaluate. (thinking out loud here)

author: 0xrubo
content: <@774033563732541451> are there any test vectors for statelessinput for the latest changes?
